behavior_in_task_description.reason: All test-asserted paths (scripts/*.js, turbo.json, turbo.jsonc, package.json files), required keys (CI, RUSTC_WRAPPER, SCCACHE_*, rust-fingerprint, dependsOn, outputs, cache), and behaviors (TURBO_HASH handling, 'skipping' message) are explicitly named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what needs to exist (tasks, scripts, env vars) without dictating specific code lines or diff content.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for required script entries, env var presence, and behavior (fingerprint script writes TURBO_HASH value); alternative correct implementations would satisfy these.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute node --check, run the rust-fingerprint.js script with env vars, run prettier, and parse JSON — not plain text inspection.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests verify actual script execution outputs (e.g., fingerprint file content equals TURBO_HASH) and presence of specific scripts/env vars that cannot be satisfied by stubs.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Instruction requirements (fingerprint task, script behavior, globalEnv, globalPassThroughEnv, package.json scripts, prettier formatting) correspond to test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly named per-check with docstrings and grouped via comment headers like 'Gates (pass_to_pass, repo_tests)'.

structured_data_schema.reason: JSON/JSONC schema requirements (globalEnv array entries, tasks keys, inputs/outputs/cache fields, script names) are enumerated in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Paths and variable names (TURBO_HASH, RUSTC_WRAPPER, SCCACHE_*, rust-fingerprint, target/.rust-fingerprint) are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the files to modify (which is part of the task domain) but doesn't give exact line numbers or code snippets.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Gold diff touches only the specified turbo.jsonc, turbo.json, package.json files and adds one new script — localized to files named in instruction.
